description_long: This is a set of three vintage metal lockers, painted in a worn teal blue color with visible rust patches and scratches, suggesting years of use in a school, gym, or urban recreational facility. Each locker has a vented door with a number stenciled in white: 368, 369, and 370. The middle locker is open, revealing several shelves filled with books, a baseball bat, a baseball, and other small items, indicating its use for personal storage. The rightmost side of the unit features a poster and some stickers, adding to its urban, lived-in character. The doors are equipped with simple metal handles and keyholes, and the structure stands on four short legs. The overall appearance is utilitarian, robust, and evocative of communal or public spaces.
description: A weathered teal blue metal locker unit with three numbered doors, one open to reveal books and sports equipment, showing signs of heavy use and urban character.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three locker doors, with the middle door open to display books, a bat, and a baseball inside.
description_view_1: Left side view showing the closed door of locker 370 and the depth of the unit.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, rusted metal back of the locker unit with no visible openings.
description_view_3: Right side view featuring a poster and stickers on the side panel, with the open door of locker 368 partially visible.
